sourcePrompt: An M50 Ontos, the tiny US Marine tank destroyer, under four meters long but impossible to mistake: six long recoilless rifles mounted in two stacked banks of three on either side of a small turret atop a stubby tracked hull. The tubes overhang the vehicle in both directions, dwarfing it. Short tracks with four road wheels per side, a low boxy hull barely taller than a man. Olive-drab, odd, bristling and disproportionate.
